instruction: Execute all tasks for FIX_CODEREF_DRIFT_PREFLIGHT and update task status in communication.json as you complete each one

tasks.description: Review Source fix and regression coverage requirements
tasks.notes: Reviewed context.json, analysis.json, plan.json, and communication.json requirements for the drift-preflight fix.

tasks.description: Read src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.ts and src/pipeline/generators/index-generator.ts to confirm where absolute-versus-relative path normalization diverges.
tasks.notes: Confirmed index-generator writes project-relative paths while drift-generator previously compared raw absolute current paths against relative previous index entries.

tasks.description: Edit src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.ts so both current scan elements and previous index elements are normalized to project-relative forward-slash paths before identity maps are built.
tasks.notes: Normalized both current and previous drift elements to project-relative forward-slash paths before map construction.

tasks.description: Edit drift report output generation so added, deleted, and modified file entries also use the normalized project-relative path format.
tasks.notes: Added, deleted, and modified drift report entries now emit normalized relative file paths via normalized element copies.

tasks.description: Add a drift-generator regression test covering unchanged elements where previous index paths are relative and current state paths are absolute.
tasks.notes: Added a regression test proving mixed relative/absolute unchanged elements report zero drift.

tasks.description: Run the coderef-core drift generator test file and confirm the new regression plus existing drift behavior both pass.
tasks.notes: Ran node ../../node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run __tests__/generators/drift-generator.test.ts outside the sandbox; 2 tests passed.

tasks.description: Review Build and downstream verification requirements
tasks.notes: Reviewed downstream verification targets in coderef-workflow, including processors/coderef_preflight.py and PlanningWorkflow.analyze_project_for_planning().

tasks.description: Rebuild coderef-core so dist/src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.js reflects the source normalization fix.
tasks.notes: Ran npm run build successfully; dist/src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.js now includes the normalization helpers.

tasks.description: Repopulate coderef-workflow and confirm the regenerated drift.json no longer reports false 200% drift for an unchanged scan.
tasks.notes: Repopulated C:\Users\willh\.mcp-servers\coderef-workflow with the rebuilt populate CLI; drift generator reported 0% drift and regenerated .coderef/reports/drift.json with empty change sets.

tasks.description: Check that coderef-workflow strict planning preflight accepts the fresh populate and that analyze_project_for_planning can continue past drift validation.
tasks.notes: Ran PlanningWorkflow.analyze_project_for_planning() in coderef-workflow; the log reported 'Strict CodeRef preflight passed' and analysis continued with inventory_source='coderef_index'.

forbidden_files: server.py - DO NOT MODIFY (production MCP server)

testing_checklist: 1. Run all unit tests for the feature
success_criteria: The drift generator reports 0 changed elements for unchanged scans with mixed absolute and relative source paths.
The existing added, deleted, and modified drift behavior still passes in the test suite.
The rebuilt drift generator emits normalized relative file paths in drift.json entries.
The regenerated coderef-workflow drift report is at or below 10% when the scan is unchanged.
coderef-workflow strict preflight advances past drift validation after the repopulate.
agent_1_status: READY - Planning complete, awaiting agent assignment
agent_N_status: COMPLETE - Source fix, regression coverage, build, and downstream verification finished

handoff_prompt_template.description: Standardized prompt template for agent handoffs (STUB-015)
handoff_prompt_template.usage: Use this template when transferring work to another agent
handoff_prompt_template.template:
You are being handed off the following work:

## Context
- **Feature**: {{feature_name}}
- **Workorder**: {{workorder_id}}
- **Current Progress**: {{progress_percent}}% ({{completed}}/{{total}} tasks)

## Files
- **Plan**: coderef/working/{{feature_name}}/plan.json
- **Communication**: coderef/working/{{feature_name}}/communication.json
- **Context**: coderef/working/{{feature_name}}/context.json

## Current State
{{current_state_summary}}

## Next Tasks
{{next_tasks_list}}

## Important Notes
- Forbidden files: {{forbidden_files}}
- Success criteria: {{success_criteria}}

## Instructions
1. Read the plan.json and communication.json
2. Continue from the next pending task
3. Update task status in plan.json as you work
4. Do not modify forbidden files
5. Run tests before marking complete

variables.current_state_summary: Generated from git status and recent commits
variables.next_tasks_list: First 3 pending tasks from tasks array
